verdicts.note: Evidence confirms NotebookLM exists and is a Google AI research assistant.
Evidence references notebooklm.google.com and references to Google Cloud/AI; the website is reasonable.
Evidence confirms NotebookLM is an AI research tool with media capabilities (audio/video overviews).
Evidence confirms NotebookLM is free with a Google account, no official public API, and unofficial tools use cookie-based browser automation.
Evidence from both GitHub repos confirms no official API exists and unofficial integrations use session cookies extracted via browser automation (Chrome DevTools Protocol / Playwright).

Evidence (felloai.com, webaistack.com) states NotebookLM is free to use with any Google account. There is NO paid consumer plan or self-serve developer API for consumers. Enterprise API exists but requires partnership. The correct value should be 'self-serve-free' for consumer use, NOT 'partnership-contact-sales'.
Evidence confirms NotebookLM is free with any Google account, no public self-serve developer API for consumers, and Enterprise API is restricted via Google Cloud Discovery Engine.
Evidence confirms no official public API exists. Enterprise API is available but not self-serve.
With no official public API and only enterprise endpoints via Discovery Engine, 'narrow' is appropriate.
Evidence confirms no official public API, Enterprise API via Discovery Engine v1alpha for organizations, and unofficial reverse-engineered endpoints.

The URL provided (https://github.com/Robiton/notebooklm-mcp-cli) is NOT a dedicated Model Context Protocol server FOR NotebookLM created by Google or an official partner; it is a community-built CLI tool that reverse-engineers NotebookLM using browser automation. This is an unofficial integration, not an official MCP server. Should be 'no'.
The GitHub repo is a community CLI tool using browser automation, not an official MCP server. The URL should be dropped when exists='no'.
Evidence confirms no official public API for self-serve developer use. Enterprise API requires partnership. Unofficial methods are fragile and violate ToS. 'blocked' is correct.
Evidence confirms no official public API, Enterprise API requires partnership, and unofficial methods are fragile/ToS-violating.
All four evidence URLs are present in the provided evidence and support the claims made.
0.95 is reasonable given strong evidence from multiple sources.
All flags are supported by the evidence: no official API, enterprise-only official API, unofficial MCP exists, cookie-based auth, ToS risk, fragile integration.

revised.name: NotebookLM
revised.one_liner: NotebookLM is a free, source-grounded AI research assistant from Google with no official public API; unofficial community tools use cookie-based authentication via browser automation.

auth_methods.notes: No official public API. Unofficial integrations use session cookie extraction via browser automation (Playwright/CDP) from active Google account sessions. Authentication requires capturing cookies and CSRF tokens from a managed browser instance.

revised.self_serve_notes: NotebookLM is free to use with any Google account, but there is no public self-serve developer API for consumers. Programmatic access via the NotebookLM Enterprise API is restricted to organizations through Google Cloud via Discovery Engine v1alpha endpoints, requiring enterprise partnership.

api_surface.notes: No official public API exists. An Enterprise API is available only to Google Cloud organizations via Discovery Engine v1alpha. Community tools have reverse-engineered unofficial endpoints using browser automation and session cookies.

revised.main_blocker: No official public API is available for self-serve developer use. The only programmatic access (NotebookLM Enterprise API via Google Cloud Discovery Engine v1alpha) requires enterprise partnership and organizational Google Cloud credentials. Unofficial methods rely on fragile cookie-based browser automation that violates typical ToS and may break at any time.

evidence.supports: Confirms no official API exists; authentication uses browser cookies extracted via Chrome DevTools Protocol or Playwright from managed browser sessions.
Unofficial TypeScript wrapper uses undocumented Google RPC endpoints with storage state or environment variable-based authentication via browser automation.
NotebookLM is free to use with any Google account. No standalone paid product or self-serve developer API for consumers exists.
Confirms no public self-serve developer API for consumer users. Programmatic access is restricted to NotebookLM Enterprise API via Google Cloud.
